title: Ozothamnus cassiope
intro: Ozothamnus cassiope, also known as everlasting or paper everlasting, is a shrubby plant native to Australia, characterized by its small, white flowers and silvery-gray foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, digestive problems, and respiratory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozothamnus cassiope include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from Ozothamnus cassiope include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried flower and leaf extracts that can be used in aromatherapy and traditional medicine practices.
taxonomy: Ozothamnus cassiope is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to Australia and is often found in a variety of habitats, including coastal areas and woodlands. Ozothamnus cassiope is typically classified as a shrub, with a compact and dense growth habit. It has small, white or yellow flowers that are often arranged in clusters, and is often used in landscaping and horticulture due to its attractive foliage and blooms. The classification of Ozothamnus cassiope is further supported by its position within the larger taxonomy of the Asteraceae family.
